purpose: Sixth AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace, covering the unsupported self-mailbox command boundary that preserves a write-buf-one command without execution.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel on the triangular unsupported mailbox node.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel on the triangular unsupported mailbox node.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel on the triangular unsupported mailbox node.

layers.summary: A write-buf-one self-mailbox command is recognized as unsupported and leaves the stem node unchanged.
layers.boundary: This layer states preservation of an unresolved command only; it does not implement write-buffer semantics.

layers.summary: The unsupported mailbox node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete command-routing circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one unsupported mailbox activation.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0034 preservation boundary and adds no command execution.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested unsupported boundary.
